name: merge-review
description: Adversarial merge-readiness review that scores a diff 0-100 across security, correctness, quality and maintainability, and runs iteratively until the score clears the threshold. Two outcome modes: LOCAL (interactive) re-derives each finding, applies the attested ones (high-confidence, file:line-cited) as minimal root-cause fixes and loops until viable — contestable pure-logic findings are surfaced, never silently applied; REMOTE (diff injected by a runner) is strictly read-only and just emits the verdict + machine-readable state. Forge-agnostic context (GitHub via gh, GitLab via glab). Self-engaging & session-scoped: a pre-push gate reviews the branch THIS session is about to push (never a stale or someone else's branch); opt out per repo. The review step between ship-when-done and mr-watchdog.
